frameworks.name: ensemble perception
frameworks.one_line: group-level statistics bias representation of individual items

frameworks.name: visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: memory retention as candidate locus of ensemble bias

frameworks.name: perceptual encoding
frameworks.one_line: encoding stage as locus where group statistics bias individuals

core_question: Does ensemble average bias individual size representations during perception or during working memory retention?
key_assumptions: individual item representations can be influenced by group statistics
reversing stimulus order isolates perceptual vs memory loci
size comparison task indexes the biased representation
manipulations: presentation order of array vs single probe (memory-bias vs perceptual-bias condition)
target size relative to ensemble average size
key_findings: bias toward ensemble average size in memory-bias condition
bias magnitude does not differentiate perceptual vs memory loci
interpretations: ensemble statistics interact with individual representations during perceptual encoding
ensemble bias is not solely a memory-retention phenomenon
